learning.summary: A recovery partition is a hidden disk partition that stores files used to repair, reset, or reinstall an operating system. Many modern operating systems and computer manufacturers include one by default.
learning.explanation:
A recovery partition is typically created during operating system installation and is hidden from normal users to help prevent accidental modification. It contains recovery tools and installation files that can be used if the operating system becomes damaged.

Computer manufacturers often customize recovery partitions with their own recovery environments, allowing users to restore a system to its factory configuration without external installation media.

facts.text: Recovery partitions are commonly hidden from normal users.
A recovery partition stores files used to repair or reinstall an operating system.
Many vendors include customized recovery environments.

examples.text: A laptop fails to boot normally, so the technician starts the built-in recovery environment from the recovery partition to repair Windows.

examTips.text: Know that recovery partitions are typically hidden and contain recovery tools.

commonMistakes.text: Deleting a recovery partition without creating alternate recovery media.

scenarios.situation: Choose the recovery partition when repairing an operating system.
scenarios.expectedAction: Identify the correct concept or response.

pbqIdeas.task: Identify the correct partition to use for system recovery.

reviewNotes: Vendor recovery environment examples are curriculum enrichment beyond the explicit transcript evidence.
Promoted from authored draft as needs-review canonical content.
